name: Caipira Portuguese
notes: Caipira (SP interior, western Minas Gerais, Goiás, Mato Grosso, Mato Grosso do Sul, Paraná hinterland). Hallmark: retroflex approximant rhotic [ɻ] in coda and clusters — the 'erre caipira / erre retroflexo' (porta -> [ˈpoɻtɐ], mar -> [maɻ]; open-mid /ɔ/ in 'porta' is lexical and unmarked in spelling, so the engine derives [o]). Directly described by Amaral (1920) §6b: 'r inter e post-vocálico (arara, carta) possui um valor peculiar: é linguo-palatal e guturalizado ... a língua ... vira a extremidade para cima, sem tocá-la na abóbada palatal. Não há quase nenhuma vibração tremulante ... este r caipira assemelha-se bastante ao r inglês post-vocálico' — i.e. a non-trilled retroflex approximant. Coda /S/ stays alveolar laminodental ciciante with no chiado (Amaral §6a: 's propriamente sibilante, assobiado, e bem assim chiante, são aqui desconhecidos'). Coda /l/ historically rhotacises to r and merges into the retroflex — Amaral §22a: 'l - Em final de sílaba, muda-se em r: quarquér, papér, mér, arma'; modern urban caipira more often vocalises to [w] (kept as the primary coda-/l/ realisation, with [ɻ] as the traditional Amaral variant). Word-final /r/ elides (Amaral §23a: 'Cai, quando final de palavra: andá, muié, esquecê'). /ʎ/ vocalises to [j] (Amaral §25: 'Vocaliza-se em i: espaiado, maio, muié'; §6e: lh 'não existe no dialeto'). /t,d/ palatalisation before /i/ is retained from the pt-BR base. NB Amaral §8 records that early-20c rural caipira did NOT raise final unstressed /e/->[i] or /o/->[u] ('Não se operou aqui a permuta de e final por i ... como não se operou a de o por u'); the spec inherits the modern pt-BR reduction, which now prevails, and this historical divergence is documented rather than modelled.

sources.author: Amaral, A.
sources.title: O Dialeto Caipira
sources.notes: Foundational description of the dialect. §6b: the retroflex non-trilled 'r caipira'. §6a: laminodental coda /s/, no chiado. §22a: coda /l/ -> r rhotacism. §23a: word-final /r/ elision. §25/§6e: /ʎ/ vocalisation. §8: early-20c retention of final unstressed /e/, /o/ (no raising).

sources.author: Callou, D. & Leite, Y.
sources.title: Iniciação à fonética e à fonologia (8th ed.)

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam). DIPHTHONGS: declared so the bundled syllabifier counts nuclei correctly — without them every vowel run merges into one nucleus and co-e-lho is read as coe-lho, one syllable short, putting the stress and its vowel reduction on the wrong vowel. ⟨oe⟩ is hiatus, not a diphthong (Cunha & Cintra, §Ditongos).

location.notes: Piracicaba — a representative locality of the caipira area, whose retroflex-rhotic region stretches from interior São Paulo across Goiás and Mato Grosso. A point badly under-describes it. [Wikidata Q330175 P625]
